name: unraid-mcp
description: Query, monitor, and manage Unraid servers via GraphQL API through MCP tools. Supports system info, Docker, VMs, array/parity, notifications, plugins, rclone, and live telemetry.

unraid_api_url.title: Unraid GraphQL API URL
unraid_api_url.description: Your Unraid server's GraphQL endpoint, e.g. https://tower.local/graphql. No trailing slash.

unraid_api_key.title: Unraid API Key
unraid_api_key.description: API key for your Unraid server. Found in Settings → Management Access → API Keys.

unraid_verify_ssl.title: Verify TLS certificate
unraid_verify_ssl.description: Controls TLS verification of the Unraid API. Leave blank to verify (the default). Set to false to disable verification for a stock self-signed cert (you must also enable "Allow insecure TLS" below), or set to a path to a CA-bundle file to trust a self-signed cert without disabling verification.

unraid_allow_insecure_tls.title: Allow insecure TLS
unraid_allow_insecure_tls.description: Required second opt-in when "Verify TLS certificate" is set to false. Disabling verification sends your API key to an unverified peer over both HTTP and WebSocket connections, exposing it to man-in-the-middle interception. Only enable this on a trusted LAN.
